text: Even a single flame can hold back the darkness.
author: Ancient Proverb

text: Prayer is not asking. It is a longing of the soul.
author: Mahatma Gandhi

text: Where there is love, there is life.
author: Mahatma Gandhi

text: The wound is the place where the light enters you.
author: Rumi

text: Faith is the bird that feels the light when the dawn is still dark.
author: Rabindranath Tagore

text: Healing takes courage, and we all have courage, even if we have to dig a little to find it.
author: Tori Amos

text: When you light a lamp for someone, it also brightens your own path.
author: Buddhist Proverb

text: Hope is being able to see that there is light despite all of the darkness.
author: Desmond Tutu

text: The prayer of the heart is the source of all good, refreshing the soul as rain refreshes the earth.
author: St. Gregory of Nyssa

text: In the middle of difficulty lies opportunity.
author: Albert Einstein

text: What lies behind us and what lies before us are tiny matters compared to what lies within us.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: Every prayer is a conversation between love and hope.
author: Unknown

text: Stars cannot shine without darkness.
author: D.H. Sidebottom

text: A candle loses nothing by lighting another candle.
author: James Keller

text: Let nothing disturb you, let nothing frighten you. All things are passing. Patience obtains all things.
author: St. Teresa of Avila

text: Out of suffering have emerged the strongest souls.
author: Khalil Gibran

text: The human spirit is stronger than anything that can happen to it.
author: C.C. Scott

text: When the world says give up, hope whispers try it one more time.
author: Unknown

text: You are not a drop in the ocean. You are the entire ocean in a drop.
author: Rumi

text: There are two ways of spreading light: to be the candle or the mirror that reflects it.
author: Edith Wharton

text: Prayers go up, blessings come down.
author: Yiddish Proverb

text: Courage does not always roar. Sometimes it is the quiet voice at the end of the day saying I will try again tomorrow.
author: Mary Anne Radmacher

text: The soul always knows what to do to heal itself. The challenge is to silence the mind.
author: Caroline Myss

text: Every moment is a fresh beginning.
author: T.S. Eliot

text: Darkness cannot drive out darkness; only light can do that.
author: Martin Luther King Jr.

text: To heal is to touch with love that which we previously touched with fear.
author: Stephen Levine

text: God is closest to those with broken hearts.
author: Jewish Proverb

text: A prayer in its simplest definition is merely a wish turned heavenward.
author: Phillips Brooks

text: Miracles happen every day. Not just in remote lands or in times long past, but here and now.
author: Deepak Chopra

text: He who has health has hope, and he who has hope has everything.
author: Arabian Proverb

text: Love and compassion are necessities, not luxuries. Without them humanity cannot survive.
author: Dalai Lama

text: Be strong enough to stand alone, smart enough to know when you need help, and brave enough to ask for it.
author: Unknown

text: The greatest healing therapy is friendship and love.
author: Hubert H. Humphrey

text: No act of kindness, no matter how small, is ever wasted.
author: Aesop

text: Sometimes the most powerful thing you can do is simply hold someone in your prayers.
author: Unknown

text: Believe you can and you are halfway there.
author: Theodore Roosevelt

text: The best way out is always through.
author: Robert Frost

text: Where hope grows, miracles blossom.
author: Elna Rae

text: A family that prays together, heals together.
author: Unknown

text: You never know how strong you are until being strong is the only choice you have.
author: Bob Marley
